title: Hazelnut praline cookies
subtitle: Buttery rounds sandwiched with smooth hazelnut praline cream
excerpt: Two thin, crisp hazelnut butter biscuits sandwiched with a smooth praline cream made from caramelised hazelnuts. The biscuits are crisp; the filling is rich, nutty, and barely sweet.
sourceNotes: Nut-paste-filled sandwich biscuits are well documented across European patisserie and confectionery traditions. The combination of ground hazelnuts in the biscuit dough with a caramelised hazelnut filling appears in French and German baking literature of the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries. This recipe follows the classic approach: a hazelnut sablé dough and a praline buttercream filling.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unfilled biscuits in an airtight container. Fill after thawing.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles easily. The praline paste can be made in larger batches and kept refrigerated for up to 2 weeks.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The biscuits keep in a tin for 5 days. Fill on the day of serving; filled cookies soften overnight.

glossaryTerms.definition: Hazelnuts (or almonds) coated in hot caramel, allowed to set, then ground in a food processor until they release their oils and become a smooth, rich paste. The caramel and nut oils combine into something close to a spreadable nut butter with a deep toffee flavour. It keeps refrigerated for 2 weeks.

content.text: The filling here is a simple

beaten with butter. It takes about 10 minutes in a food processor to go from a coarse crumb to a smooth, spreadable paste; stop before it becomes completely liquid or the filling will ooze. The biscuits are a standard hazelnut sablé: crisp, short, and rich.

Beat the butter and icing sugar until pale. Beat in the egg yolk. Mix in the flour, ground hazelnuts, and salt until the dough just comes together. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Line a baking tray with baking paper. Heat the sugar in a dry heavy pan over medium heat without stirring until it melts to a deep amber caramel. Remove from heat and stir in the toasted hazelnuts. Pour onto the lined tray and spread flat. Allow to set hard, about 20 minutes. Break into pieces and blend in a food processor, scraping down the sides, until a smooth paste forms. Beat the praline paste with the softened butter until smooth and spreadable.

Preheat the oven to 170°C conventional. Roll the chilled dough to 4 mm thick on a lightly floured surface. Cut into rounds with a 6 cm cutter. Re-roll the trimmings once only. Bake on lined trays for 10 to 12 minutes until pale gold. Transfer to a rack and cool completely. Spread a generous teaspoon of praline cream on the flat side of half the biscuits and sandwich with the remaining halves, pressing gently.
